advisories_at_spawn.title: PyO3 Risk of buffer overflow in `PyString::from_object`
PyO3 Risk of buffer overflow in `PyString::from_object` (second range entry)
js-yaml has prototype pollution in merge (<<)
Specially crafted inputs to idna.encode() can bypass CVE-2024-3651 fix

advisories_resolved.resolution: Bumped pyo3 0.22.6 → 0.24.1 in src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock. NOTE: pyo3-0.28.3 (higher Dependabot branch) was evaluated first but requires binding-code changes (get_type_bound removed in 0.28.x) — out-of-scope per ADR-0.6.0-no-shims-policy § 54. pyo3 0.24.1 resolves the advisory with zero API surface impact (deprecated method warnings only, no errors).

js-yaml 4.1.0 → 4.1.1 via markdownlint-cli2 bump ^0.18.0 → ^0.22.1 (npm audit fix --force). Advisory GHSA-38c4-r59v-3vqw (markdown-it ReDoS) also resolved by same bump. Dev-tooling-only dependency (markdown linting); zero fathomdb API surface impact.

idna is NOT a transitive dependency of the Python SDK environment (deps: pytest, hypothesis, ruff, pyright). uv.lock created via 'uv sync --extra dev' (15 packages, no idna entry). 'uv lock --upgrade-package idna' confirmed no change needed. Dependabot manifestFile path 'python/uv.lock' corresponds to src/python/uv.lock (created in this slice). pip-audit shows 0 vulnerabilities.

pyo3_0_28_3_evaluation.reason: pyo3 0.28.x removed get_type_bound() (renamed back to get_type()). fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs uses get_type_bound at lines 643-645. Code change would be required — out of scope per ADR-0.6.0-no-shims-policy § 54 patch-release constraint. Fallback to 0.24.1 applied.
additional_0_28_blockers_per_codex_2026_: Python::with_gil(...) → Python::attach(...) rename: 5 call sites in fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs (codex review at marker.rs:410 + marker.rs:648 in pyo3-0.28.3). The 0.28.x migration will need to rewrite these too, not just the get_type_bound block at L643-645. Initial output.json text understated the migration scope; this entry corrects the record so 0.7.0 perf-slice planners know the actual surface area when the pyo3 bump is paired with their work.
pyo3_0_28_3_evaluation.future_migration_owner: 0.7.0 perf slice OR a dedicated 0.6.x pyo3-bump slice if Dependabot re-surfaces a fresh pyo3 advisory before 0.7.0 ships. Not in 0.6.1 scope (patch contract).

npm.note: Checked both root package (fathomdb-dev-tooling) and src/ts (fathomdb TypeScript SDK)

commit_shas.message: fix(security/python): add uv.lock; idna advisory addressed (CVE-2024-3651)

api_surface_impact: NONE — all changes are lockfile/manifest version bumps of dev or transitive dependencies. pyo3 0.24.1 upgrade is backward-compatible at the fathomdb Python API surface (get_type_bound deprecated but functional, no user-visible change). markdownlint-cli2 is dev tooling only. idna not present in Python SDK deps.
notes: pyo3 0.28.3 Dependabot PR evaluated and rejected: requires get_type_bound → get_type code change in fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs. This is 0.7.x scope per no-shims policy. pyo3 0.24.1 resolves the advisory.
npm audit also flagged markdown-it GHSA-38c4-r59v-3vqw (ReDoS) — resolved by same markdownlint-cli2 upgrade.

idna Python advisory: not present in project deps. uv.lock created in this slice (file did not previously exist in repo). Dependabot manifestFile path mismatch (reports 'python/uv.lock', actual path 'src/python/uv.lock') — advisory may require manual close on GitHub.
